sketch2.title: Practice the core idea of Removing Duplicates

concept_overview.title: What is Removing Duplicates?
concept_overview.bodyMarkdown:
Removing Duplicates focuses on this idea: Combining DISTINCT and string cleanup.

A good starting point is to say the requirement in plain language before writing SQL.

### Learning goals
- remove duplicate text entries
- Practice the core idea of Removing Duplicates

goal_one.title: remove duplicate text entries
goal_one.bodyMarkdown:
remove duplicate text entries.

When practicing Removing Duplicates, start with a focused example and verify the result carefully.

goal_two.title: Practice the core idea of Removing Duplicates
goal_two.bodyMarkdown:
Practice the core idea of Removing Duplicates.

Use the topic idea to explain what the query is doing in plain English.

practice_note.title: How to practice Removing Duplicates
practice_note.bodyMarkdown:
Read the requirement, predict the result, run the query, and verify whether the output really answers the question.

That habit is especially important for Removing Duplicates.

module4_removing_duplicates_practice_sql.title: Practice: Removing Duplicates
module4_removing_duplicates_practice_sql.prompt:
The `products` table already exists.

Write a query that shows the `name` and `price` columns for rows whose `price` is greater than `50`.

Then run it.
module4_removing_duplicates_practice_sql.hint: Use WHERE with a comparison operator.
module4_removing_duplicates_practice_sql.starterCode:
-- Filter rows by price
SELECT name, price
FROM products
WHERE price > 50

module4_removing_duplicates_core_concept.title: Core idea: Removing Duplicates
module4_removing_duplicates_core_concept.prompt: What is the best description of Removing Duplicates?

help.hint_1: Look for the option that matches the main concept directly.
help.hint_2: Ignore answers that describe unrelated SQL behavior.

module4_removing_duplicates_core_concept.hint: Match the answer to the topic summary.

options.a: It keeps only the rows that match a condition.

module4_removing_duplicates_true_stateme.title: True statements about Removing Duplicates
module4_removing_duplicates_true_stateme.prompt: Select all true statements about Removing Duplicates.

help.hint_1: Pick statements that align with the topic summary and objectives.
help.hint_2: Reject statements that clearly describe unrelated SQL ideas.

module4_removing_duplicates_true_stateme.hint: Use the summary and objectives as your guide.

options.a: Filtering is often done with WHERE.
options.b: Comparison operators help define the row condition.
options.c: Filtering always changes the stored table data.
options.d: AND and OR can change which rows stay in the output.
options.e: Filtering is unrelated to business questions.

module4_removing_duplicates_workflow_ord.title: Workflow for Removing Duplicates
module4_removing_duplicates_workflow_ord.prompt: Reorder the workflow ideas for Removing Duplicates.

help.concept: Identify which rows should stay, write the condition, then verify the result.
help.hint_1: Think about what should happen first.
help.hint_2: Then think about what should happen last.

tokens.t1: decide which rows should stay in the result
tokens.t2: write the condition in the WHERE clause
tokens.t3: run the query and verify the remaining rows

module4_removing_duplicates_keyword_fill.title: Filtering keyword
module4_removing_duplicates_keyword_fill.prompt: Fill in the blank with the best SQL keyword.

help.hint_1: Think about which SQL term best completes the sentence.
help.hint_2: Use the topic meaning to eliminate unrelated options.

module4_removing_duplicates_keyword_fill.hint: Match the sentence to the right SQL term.
module4_removing_duplicates_keyword_fill.template: The clause commonly used to filter rows is ____.
